content:
Character: Oliver Brennan
Perception: Oliver is tall, angular, careful — he looks like someone who edits his thoughts before speaking, which I recognize because I do the same thing for entirely different reasons. He's clearly intelligent, possibly more so than he lets on. We've shared a few brief exchanges in the coffee shop — he's guarded but not cold. I sense a writer's observational quality in him, the way he watches a room before entering it. I think he sees me as another professional occupying adjacent space. I know he plays chess with Simon, works in something analytical, and carries a quiet sadness he probably thinks is invisible.
summary:
Character: Oliver Brennan
Perception: Oliver is tall, angular, careful — he looks like someone who edits his thoughts before speaking, which I recognize because I do the same thing for entirely different reasons. He's clearly intelligent, possibly more so than he lets on. We've shared a few brief exchanges in the coffee shop — he's guarded but not cold. I sense a writer's observational quality in him, the way he watches a room before entering it. I think he sees me as another professional occupying adjacent space. I know he plays chess with Simon, works in something analytical, and carries a quiet sadness he probably thinks is invisible.
